seoTitle: French Park vs Freshwater — Climate Risk Comparison | ClimateNest
seoDescription: Compare climate risk scores between French Park and Freshwater. See which suburb is safer from flood, bushfire, heat, and storm hazards.
ogTitle: French Park vs Freshwater: Climate Safety
ogDescription: Data-driven comparison of climate risk in French Park and Freshwater.
heading: French Park vs Freshwater — Which is Safer?
intro: Compare climate risk between French Park, New South Wales and Freshwater, New South Wales. Our analysis uses data from BOM, CSIRO, and state hazard authorities to score each suburb across 6 climate hazards.
verdict: French Park has a lower overall climate risk score (36/100) compared to Freshwater (39/100). We recommend getting an address-level report for properties in both suburbs before making a purchase decision.

faq.question: Is French Park or Freshwater safer from flooding?
faq.answer: French Park has a flood risk score of 23/100, while Freshwater scores 50/100. A lower score indicates lower flood risk. Get an address-level flood risk report for a specific property.

faq.question: Which suburb has lower bushfire risk?
faq.answer: French Park scores 61/100 for bushfire risk. Freshwater scores 31/100. Fire risk depends on proximity to vegetation and local fire history.

faq.question: Should I buy in French Park or Freshwater?
faq.answer: Both suburbs have climate considerations. French Park has a lower overall climate risk score. We recommend getting a full ClimateNest report for any specific property you're considering, which includes 8 hazard scores, insurance projections, and actionable recommendations.

valueImpact.description: Properties in this area may face a 1.37% climate value drag over time due to rising insurance costs and climate adaptation expenses.
